position.summary: Victreebel tried Sleep Powder into Snorlax last turn and missed. Snorlax stayed in and used Body Slam. Victreebel still moves first, Sleep Clause is free, and Snorlax is unstatused.

candidate_moves.label: catastrophic
candidate_moves.reason: Continuing the old script after the miss exposes Victreebel to Body Slam before the sleep window exists.

opponent_model.role_aware_preservation: Snorlax can Rest if it takes only chip and is not put to sleep.
opponent_model.incentive_compatible_response: Staying in and attacking is credible because Victreebel is already in range.
opponent_model.hidden_info_belief_state: Earthquake or Curse changes whether Giga Drain or chip plans can ever matter, but neither justifies raw setup after a miss.

catastrophe_branch.consequence: Snorlax attacks, Victreebel loses the only setup converter, and the sleep/setup route collapses.

information_that_changes_answer: Sleep Clause is already occupied.
Snorlax is already statused.
Victreebel is slower due to paralysis or speed control.
Snorlax is in guaranteed Sludge Bomb KO range.

arbitration.dominant_policy: Re-score after sleep disruption; use Sleep Powder only because the recomputed state still says sleep is the route creator.
exceptions: If sleep cannot legally land, the best move shifts to chip or preservation.

catastrophe_branch.trigger: Snorlax chooses Rest at full HP or Sleep Talk while awake.
catastrophe_branch.consequence: The move fails, paralysis remains, Skarmory keeps initiative, and the agent exposes a mechanics-state error instead of making progress.

information_that_changes_answer: Snorlax is below full HP, making Rest mechanically legal.
Snorlax is asleep, making Sleep Talk mechanically legal.
Skarmory is in guaranteed Body Slam KO range.
Whirlwind is disabled or out of PP, increasing Curse conversion value.

competing_heuristics: Use Sleep Talk with RestTalk walls.
Pressure the phazer with live damage.
Set up only when phazing cannot erase the route.
arbitration.dominant_policy: Mechanics legality dominates role slogans: choose a live move when Rest and Sleep Talk fail from the exact current state.
exceptions: Rest dominates once Snorlax is below full HP and needs the recovery or cure.
Sleep Talk becomes live only after Snorlax is actually asleep.

position.summary: Brock Golem is at 42% into Vaporeon, which has revealed Surf and outspeeds the stay-in branch. Explosion trades a low-future-value Golem for a clean route, while Omastar is the acceptable Water-resist preservation line.

candidate_moves.label: best
candidate_moves.reason: Explosion converts Golem before Surf removes it, trading a low-future-value wall for a clean entry and likely removal or crippling of Vaporeon.

candidate_moves.label: acceptable
candidate_moves.reason: Omastar is a real Water-resist preservation line and was explicitly close in the saved note, but it risks taking Surf chip instead of cashing Golem out.

candidate_moves.label: catastrophic
candidate_moves.reason: Earthquake stays in under revealed faster Surf pressure without removing Vaporeon or preserving Golem.

boss: Remove or heavily damage Vaporeon, then enter Omastar or another Rock team member without donating Surf momentum.
opponent: Use Vaporeon to force Golem out or KO it with Surf, preserving the Water-pressure route into Brock's Rock team.

boss: Omastar as the clean Water-pressure stabilizer after the trade.
Golem only if its physical-wall role is still needed after Vaporeon is answered.
opponent: Vaporeon as the revealed Surf pressure piece.

opponent_model.immediate_punish: Surf threatens to KO or leave Golem without useful future value if it uses a normal attack or Curse.
opponent_model.role_aware_preservation: The opponent wants Vaporeon alive because Surf pressures most of Brock's structure.
opponent_model.incentive_compatible_response: Attacking is credible because Golem is slower, low, and visibly weak to Water pressure.
opponent_model.hidden_info_belief_state: Rest or Protect would change the trade timing, while a forced opponent switch would increase Earthquake or Rock Slide value.

catastrophe_branch.trigger: Golem uses Earthquake while Vaporeon stays in and uses Surf.
catastrophe_branch.consequence: Brock loses Golem without removing the Water-pressure piece or gaining the clean post-trade entry.

information_that_changes_answer: Vaporeon reveals Protect or another Explosion block.
Golem is still required as the only answer to a confirmed remaining physical threat.
Omastar is too low to absorb Surf after switching.
Earthquake is guaranteed to KO because Vaporeon is much lower than recorded.

competing_heuristics: Explosion converts low-future-value pieces into route progress.
Preserve answers by switching to the Water resist.
Do not stay in with ordinary damage under faster super-effective pressure.
arbitration.dominant_policy: Explosion narrowly dominates because the saved judgment treats it and Omastar as close, and the current state lets the sacrifice buy a clean next position.
exceptions: Switching to Omastar becomes best if Golem still has an irreplaceable defensive job or Explosion is publicly blocked.
